channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. The first JDK 24 release candidate arrived February 6, following two rampdown stages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: These groups are pushing for the NFL to end facial recognition
item.description: Ahead of Super Bowl Sunday, online privacy groups Fight for the Future and the Algorithmic Justice League are reiterating a call for the NFL to put an end to the use of facial recognition in football stadiums, where the groups say the technology is used to authenticate employees, vendors, and author
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Can’t buy an Nvidia RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU due to stock shortages? If you were hoping GeForce Now might provide a good alternative, we’ve got some bad news
item.description: Nvidia GeForce Now goes from bad to worse for sign-ups, with no plans available at all, now – but day passes will return in a couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups
item.description: GSA senior leaders are saying the Trump administration’s long-term goal is to merge GSA, OPM and OMB into a single agency. The post GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: House lawmakers push to ban AI app DeepSeek from US government devices
item.description: Lawmakers in the House are proposing to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from U.S. government devices.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oracle’s HR software now has AI agents to help with taxes and career planning
item.description: Oracle’s new AI agents will answer employee questions about everything job-related, from hiring to retiring.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump's Hiring Freeze Threatens Cyber Workforce Efforts - Federal News Network
item.description: Lawmakers and experts express concern that the Trump administration's hiring freeze will hinder recent initiatives to recruit cyber and technology personnel into government.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Using NATS with .NET Aspire
item.description: If you’re building a cloud-native application using a scalable cluster of microservices, you’re going to need a way for those services to communicate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk says DOGE will make 'rapid safety upgrades' to air traffic control system
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk on Wednesday said the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) will make "rapid safety upgrades" to the air traffic control systems with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Malicious package found in the Go ecosystem – update
item.description: Update February 6: Google has removed the package and provided the following statement: The module has been removed from both the Go module proxy and GitHub, and we’ve added it to the Go vulnerability database for anyone who thinks they may have been impacted.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: With ‘Air,’ Opera launches a browser to reduce stress
item.description: If you get stressed out when surfing the web, Norwegian browser company Opera has something for you: a new browser for Windows and Mac designed to help you relax. Opera Air offers a minimalist design and scheduled breaks for breathing exercises, neck training and meditation, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI takes big step forward with 3D-printed shoe
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How to minimize your digital footprint when you travel
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to dramatically reduce the risk of having your information stolen while booking flights, cruises, vehicles and accommodations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Linux containers in 2025 and beyond
item.description: The use of Linux containers has shown no sign of slowing down since their emergence in the early 1980s.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco researchers highlight emerging threats to AI models
item.description: Cisco security researchers this week detailed a number of threats they are seeing from bad actors trying to infect or attack AI’s most common component – the large language model.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order to create US sovereign wealth fund, eyeing TikTok purchase
item.description: President Trump on Monday directed secretaries to begin a process to create an American sovereign wealth fund, suggesting it could partially own the popular app TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
